user_input: What is the condition for reaching a solution with E[L(θt)] − L∗ ≤ ϵ after a certain number of iterations?

reference_context: Since tr(Σ(θt)) ≤ α(L(θt) − L∗), we have E[L(θt+1) | θt] ≤ L(θt) − ηµ(L(θt) − L∗) + η2ℓα/2B (L(θt) − L∗). Choosing η = min(1/ℓ, µB/ℓα), we obtain E[L(θt+1)] − L∗ ≤ (1 − min(µ/2ℓ, µ2B/2ℓα))(E[L(θt)] − L∗). Therefore we reach a solution after t = max(2ℓ/µ, 2ℓα/µ2B) log(L(θ0) − L∗/ϵ) iterations.
